title: hoya gutierrezii subsp. gutierrezii
intro: Hoya gutierrezii subsp. gutierrezii, also known as the 'Mexican Hoya', is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Mexico and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Hoya gutierrezii subsp. gutierrezii include relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
